title: Understanding the Forces
text: To find the linear acceleration, consider the forces acting on the cylinder. The gravitational force can be split into components parallel and perpendicular to the incline.

title: Components of Gravitational Force
text: The component of gravitational force parallel to the incline is $$m g sin\left(\theta\right)$$, where theta is the angle of the incline.

title: Frictional Force
text: The frictional force opposes the motion and is given by $$f_k=\mu_k N$$, where N is the normal force and $$\mu_k$$ is the coefficient of kinetic friction.

title: Normal Force
text: The normal force N is equal to the component of gravitational force perpendicular to the incline, which is $$m g cos\left(\theta\right)$$.

title: Net Force and Acceleration
text: The net force acting on the cylinder is the difference between the parallel component of gravitational force and the frictional force. Use $$F=ma$$ to find the linear acceleration.

title: Calculate Linear Acceleration
text: Using the equation $$a=g sin\left(\theta\right)-\mu_k g cos\left(\theta\right)$$, calculate the linear acceleration.
